Treasurer submit immediately all the financial reports and accounts required
from their offices as indicated in Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160 and
Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs. The prompt and regular submission of these
reports must always be given emphasis in order that the analysis,
examination of accounts, post audit and proper verification of the
transactions will be conducted.

and address the same in order to comply with the required submission dates
of accounts and reports.

1.11 Henceforth, Management exert all necessary efforts to render the agency’s
accounts within the prescribed period.

2.12 We recommended that the:
2.12.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an actual
and yearly physical count of local road networks, and prepare and
submit the RPCLRN to COA Office for verification and reconciliation
with the complete and updated LRNLC and the LRNPC to be
maintained by the Accounting Office and General Service Office,
respectively;
2.12.2 Management invite a representative of the Auditor to witness the
physical count;
2.12.3 The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Mayor or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe, with
the complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects; and
2.12.4 The Municipal Mayor or the Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe,
maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and keep a complete
Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
2.12.5 The Municipal Accountant –
2.12.5.1 Trace the recorded amounts of roads, highways and bridges,
which were closed to government equity in the prior years in
its books of accounts, to comply with the required transition
provision (75% of road networks at the end of 2018/ 100% of
road networks at the end of 2019), keep and maintain Registry
of Public Infrastructures for Road and subsidiary records for
roads and road components for every road network and
provide for the depreciation for the local road network;
2.12.5.2 Include in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary details
of the local roads recorded in the books as prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

3.5.1 Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee to conduct an actual
and yearly inventory taking, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE to
COA Office for verification and reconciliation with the complete
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and the
Property Cards maintained by the Accounting Office and Property
Unit, respectively;
3.5.2 Management invite a representative of the Auditor to witness the
inventory taking;
3.5.3 The Accounting Office and Property Unit maintain and update PPELC,
Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) and Property Cards,
respectively, for each inventory item to promote effective monitoring
and controls over PPE; and
3.5.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian reconcile with
the RPCPPE and verify variances for appropriate action and
subsequent adjustments as necessary.

establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and furnish

following the procedures stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

ensure that all transactions are included in the financial statements.

report and supporting documents.

infrastructure projects must be in strict compliance with the

17.4 We recommended that Management clarify the mode of procurement used
by the LGU and submit the BAC Resolutions recommending the use of
alternative mode of procurement, ABC, and other necessary documents
depending on the specific alternative mode of procurement as required by
R.A. No. 9184.

18.5 We recommended that Management submit written justification/s on the
above noted deficiency. Henceforth, fully use the PhilGEPS in accordance
with the policies, rules, regulations, and procedures adopted by the GPPB
and embodied in the Government Procurement Reform Act.

19.6 We recommended that the Management submit justification/s on the above-
noted lapses. Henceforth, utilization of the 20% Development Fund be
limited on programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the
attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures
and yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

20.3 We recommended that Management:
20.3.1 Thoroughly plan for the 20% Development Fund Investment Plan; and
20.3.2 Ensure that all priority projects identified by the Local Development
Council be implemented in order to achieve desired goals and
consequently elevate the quality of life of its constituents.

21.4 We recommended that Management utilize the unexpended balance of allocated
funds for the intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still
priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and to
accordingly re-appropriate the funds of PPA which have become irrelevant or
not responsive to current priority undertakings.

22.7 We recommended that Management comply strictly with the provisions of
R.A. No. 10121, COA Circular No. 2012-02 and the related subsequent
provisions on LDRRMF investment planning and utilization. Henceforth,
Management submit the approved LDRRMFP with details as to projects
and activities and costs, for audit/evaluation.

23.5 In view of the above noted observations, we recommended that
Management strictly follow the LDRRMP and in case of any changes, a re-
alignment should be enacted first before its implementation to serve as an
authority for the Local Chief Executive to enter into contract in behalf of
the LGU in accordance to Section 22 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 and the applicable
jurisprudence. Henceforth, Management carefully plan for the PPAs that
can be implemented in order to prepare the Municipality from the disasters
and other events, with strict adherence with the provisions of NDRRMC-
DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, the Local
Government Code and the applicable jurisprudence, as aid in the
preparation of Municipality’s LDRMMP.

24.8 We recommended that Management submit justification for non-
implementation of the programs, projects and activities. Henceforth, ensure
that Special Education Fund be spent specifically in fulfillment of the
purpose for which the fund was created as determined and approved by the
Local School Board.

25.8 In view of the above noted lapses, we recommended to Management the
following measures to be undertaken:
25.8.1 Submit copy of the final version of the SWMP, with a Sangguniang
Bayan Resolution and an annual progress report on the strategies
implemented, as well as the accomplishments to the NSWMC, and
furnish copy to this Office for audit/evaluation;
25.8.2 The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as
to the reason for the non-monitoring of implementation of the SWM
projects and the non-submission of the accomplishment report; and
25.8.3 Revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth,
implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to facilitate an
efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in
the SWMP.

26.4 We have recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit GPB to the
PPDO of the Provincial Government to ensure the alignment of the
municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province, then from PPDO
to the DILG Regional Offices for review and approval and submit to COA
for audit/evaluation.

27.5 We recommended that the Municipal Government identify the GAD PPAs
through the use of HGDG which would be the basis in the budget
determination depending on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD
interventions.

28.4 We recommended that the Municipal Government ensure the appropriation
of at least 5% of the total budget appropriations as authorized under the
General Appropriations Act for gender and development activities.

29.3 We recommended that the GAD focal person submit the required GAD
Accomplishment Report consistent with the provisions of PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, accompanied with
the reports/documents as required by the same circular, and the DILG -
approved/-reviewed GPB to COA to facilitate audit of GAD Fund
utilization.

30.2 We recommended that the management establish and/or strengthen GAD
monitoring and evaluation systems to assess the effectiveness and efficiency
of their gender mainstreaming efforts, and the M&E activities shall be
included in the annual GPB.

31.7 We recommended that Management strictly comply with remittance of the
withheld taxes within ten days following the end of the month the
withholding taxes made as required in Section 114(c) of the NIRC to avoid
penalty for the violation as sanctioned under Section 272 of the same code.

32.4 We recommended that Management submit the printout of email messages
that the submitted alphalist has been successfully uploaded to the BIR data
warehouse and the printed copy of the computer screen display of the
acknowledgment/confirmation of the BIRfs receipt of the alphalist and
attached the said documents to the alphalist attached in the Journal Entry
Vouchers, for audit/verification. Henceforth, faithfully monitor records
pertaining to withholding and remittance of taxes and ensure that the same
is consistent with relevant tax laws and regulations.

33.5 In view of the noted deficiencies, we recommended that Management:
33.5.1 Settle immediately the under and over-remittances to the GSIS and
the officials and employees concerned, and submit explanation on the
late remittances;
33.5.2 Trace back transactions to identify the municipal officials/employees
the prior year/s withholdings were due to properly remit the said
premium contributions/loan payments withheld as mandated by laws,
rules and regulations. Interest and surcharges for delayed remittance
shall be borne by the erring officials and employees; and
33.5.3 Henceforth, remit all contributions and loan amortizations due to
GSIS within the reglementary period to avoid imposition of sanctions,
fines and penalties.

34.6 We recommended that the personnel-in-charge of payroll, Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit to COA
additional documents and computations to establish the reliability and
accuracy of the journal entries drawn.
